hypothesis.strategy: Expand the 4 lowest-scoring articles (tracking-ai-research, foundation-models-for-research, aider, blockchain-ai-optimization) with overview/background sections, fix broken links via path normalization, add cross-category links, and add sourced footnotes with 2025-2026 papers
hypothesis.reasoning: Previous session showed background sections are highest-leverage additions (+35 for institutions-and-labs). Broken links in consistency scorer penalize -3 per link. Cross-category linking gaps leave 30 points on the table per article. Targeting the 4 lowest-scoring articles maximizes composite impact due to tier-weighted averaging.

edits: tracking-ai-research: Added overview + background sections, 7 footnoted references, 10 cross-category links, fixed broken sibling links, added Current State and Limitations sections

foundation-models-for-research: Added Background/Theoretical Foundations section with 6 footnoted references, added cross-links to research-sources and frontier-topics, fixed consistency-checker link paths
aider: Added Background section, Current State, Limitations, 5 footnoted references, cross-links to all 5 categories
blockchain-ai-optimization: Added Current State section with 4 sourced developments, cross-links to methodologies and research-sources, converted bare references to footnotes
predictive-simulation-learning: Added 2 new papers (RLVR-World, UK tutoring RCT, LLM-based tutor training) to Current State
Created ai-learning-pipeline.svg diagram for tracking-ai-research article

result.lesson: Targeting the 4 lowest-scoring standard/reference articles with background sections, footnotes, and cross-links yielded +4.2 composite points exactly as predicted. The consistency checker's link resolution from frontier-topics/ requires using ../category/ path format for sibling links in non-frontier-topics articles. Aider moved from stub to standard with just background + current state sections.
